title: Hevea cuneata
taxonomy: Hevea cuneata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It belongs to the genus Hevea, which includes several other species of trees native to the Amazon rainforest. The Euphorbiaceae family consists of over 280 genera and approximately 7,500 species of plants, many of which are found in tropical regions. These plants are known for their diverse range of shapes and sizes, as well as their ability to thrive in a variety of environments. Hevea cuneata is a relatively lesser-known species within this family, but it is still an important part of the ecosystem in its native habitat.

The latex of Hevea cuneata contains compounds that have been studied for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
